trivia_bullets.text: Dub was first printed in Dominaria (DOM, May 2018) before being reprinted in Core Set 2021 (M21, July 2020). Its flavor text roots it in New Benalia — the reconstituted Benalish nation organized around seven knightly Houses and the Church of Serra.

Dub has exactly two printings (Dominaria no. 15 and Core Set 2021 no. 16) with identical art by Bastien L. Deharme across both. The type-granting effect — turning any creature into a Knight — is the card's mechanical identity: it exists to enable Knight synergies in Dominaria's Knight-heavy white.

"Rise, knight of New Benalia." The speaker is unnamed but the phrasing is a formal conferral of knighthood. New Benalia is Benalia rebuilt on Dominaria as a military-mercantile power after the Phyrexian catastrophe.

Artist Bastien L. Deharme (full name: Bastien Lecouffe Deharme) is a French digital artist known for dark atmospheric painterly imagery. Dub is notably quieter in register than his typical work. No dedicated Hipsters of the Coast feature was found.
EDHREC rank 18,112 indicates low Commander uptake. Primary home is budget Pauper and Pioneer Knight-tribal lists. Legal in Pauper, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander.

related_cards.name: Knight of New Benalia
related_cards.note: Fellow New Benalia Knight; shares the plane's Knight-tribal identity this card enables

related_cards.name: History of Benalia
related_cards.note: Saga depicting the long arc of Benalia's knightly tradition; thematic anchor for the New Benalia world this card invokes

related_cards.name: Benalish Marshal
related_cards.note: Premier Knight anthem in the same set; the deck Dub slots into
